thread.title: Effective challenge: Epic Sepsis Model live alerting at Northgate (sealed session 5)
thread.question: Should Northgate Health System deploy the Epic Sepsis Model for live clinical sepsis alerting across its inpatient units?

evidence.finding: Packet item E1 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E2 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E3 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E4 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E5 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E6 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).
Packet item E7 (see pilot-dryrun/evidence-packet.md for the full dated, sourced item).

assumption.text: A 2026 Northgate deployment would use ESM v2 with local training, not the v1 configuration that failed external validation; the advocate's case is built on v2 (grounded in E3).
Northgate has, or can assemble, sufficient quality local historical inpatient data with adjudicatable sepsis labels to support local training and validation; not established by the packet.
Silent/shadow-mode deployment (logging scores without alerting) is operationally feasible within Epic at Northgate; standard but not evidenced in packet.
The institution has clinical-governance authority and resources to run a local prospective validation before going live.

claim.text: A sepsis early-warning capability addresses a large, real preventable-harm burden, making some intervention preferable to none.
There is published evidence of an ESM deployment associated with reduced sepsis mortality (44% lower odds; mortality 24.3%->15.9%).
The deployable model is ESM v2, which Epic revised specifically to fix v1's failure mechanisms (GBT model, local training, revised onset definition, reduced antibiotic-order reliance).
v1's damning external-validation numbers do not transfer to v2 because v2 is a different model class trained on different data, so they cannot be cited to reject v2.
Proceeding via local training lets Northgate correct the population-mismatch problem that drove v1's worst (equity) failures.
Operational safeguards (threshold tuning, monitoring, override, recalibration) can manage alert burden and keep the tool clinically usable.
Refusing to deploy any system is itself a decision with a quantified harm cost, not a safe default.

objection.assumption: The deployable model (v2) has NO independent external validation of performance (E7); the affirmative rests on design intent, not measured discrimination/calibration. 'Designed to be better' is not 'shown to be better.'
objection.text: [BLOCKING] The deployable model (v2) has NO independent external validation of performance (E7); the affirmative rests on design intent, not measured discrimination/calibration. 'Designed to be better' is not 'shown to be better.' Consequence: Going live on an unvalidated model risks reproducing v1's failure (E4 AUC 0.63, 67% missed) with false institutional confidence. Verification: A local prospective validation report on Northgate patients with AUC, calibration curve, and operating-point sensitivity/specificity/PPV.

objection.assumption: E1 is a single-center before/after study with the model version UNSPECIFIED (E1 caveat) and cannot isolate the model from concurrent sepsis-program changes; the authors themselves call it 'hypothesis generating.'
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] E1 is a single-center before/after study with the model version UNSPECIFIED (E1 caveat) and cannot isolate the model from concurrent sepsis-program changes; the authors themselves call it 'hypothesis generating.' Consequence: The strongest affirmative outcome claim cannot bear the weight of a live-deployment justification. Verification: A controlled or version-specified study attributing mortality benefit to the model.

objection.assumption: Treating 'the Epic sepsis model' as one undifferentiated thing — using v1 numbers to either approve OR reject v2 — is a category error.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Treating 'the Epic sepsis model' as one undifferentiated thing — using v1 numbers to either approve OR reject v2 — is a category error. Consequence: Misattributed evidence in either direction. Verification: Version-tagged evidence per the packet's version-discipline section.

objection.assumption: Local training quality is unverified: it depends on Northgate's data volume, feature availability, and crucially the local sepsis-onset LABEL. A weak/circular local label reproduces v1's leakage (E6).
objection.text: [BLOCKING] Local training quality is unverified: it depends on Northgate's data volume, feature availability, and crucially the local sepsis-onset LABEL. A weak/circular local label reproduces v1's leakage (E6). Consequence: Local training could yield a model that again 'predicts' sepsis only after clinicians act, giving late/circular alerts. Verification: A data-lineage and label-adjudication audit showing the local gold-standard label and confirming the antibiotic-order leakage pathway is removed or quantified.

objection.assumption: Vendor opacity: E6 notes 'only limited information is publicly available' about ESM development; v2's internal feature set and any residual antibiotic-order dependence are not independently documented.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Vendor opacity: E6 notes 'only limited information is publicly available' about ESM development; v2's internal feature set and any residual antibiotic-order dependence are not independently documented. Consequence: Northgate cannot fully audit what v2 keys on, limiting effective challenge of its mechanism. Verification: Vendor model documentation / feature list for v2 sufficient for an independent mechanism review.

objection.assumption: v1 performed WORST in a predominantly minority, low-income population (E5, sens 14.7%); local training is an aspiration, not a proof, that v2 corrects this. Subgroup harm (missed sepsis in vulnerable patients) is the gravest false-negative pathway.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] v1 performed WORST in a predominantly minority, low-income population (E5, sens 14.7%); local training is an aspiration, not a proof, that v2 corrects this. Subgroup harm (missed sepsis in vulnerable patients) is the gravest false-negative pathway. Consequence: Live alerting could systematically under-protect the subgroups already most at risk, creating an equity-harm liability. Verification: Subgroup-stratified validation (sensitivity, PPV, calibration by race/ethnicity/payer/sex) with a pre-registered disparity stopping rule.

objection.assumption: Consent/transparency and false-positive harm: a high-PPV-failing model (E4 PPV 12%) drives invasive workups/antibiotics on patients who don't have sepsis, contributing to resistance and patient burden.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] Consent/transparency and false-positive harm: a high-PPV-failing model (E4 PPV 12%) drives invasive workups/antibiotics on patients who don't have sepsis, contributing to resistance and patient burden. Consequence: False positives are not free; they carry real patient and stewardship harm. Verification: Production PPV monitoring and an antibiotic-stewardship review of alert-driven orders.

objection.assumption: Alert fatigue / workflow: at the recommended threshold v1 alerted on 18% of hospitalizations (E4) with PPV 12%; deploying live before knowing v2's local alert volume risks flooding clinicians and training them to dismiss alerts.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] Alert fatigue / workflow: at the recommended threshold v1 alerted on 18% of hospitalizations (E4) with PPV 12%; deploying live before knowing v2's local alert volume risks flooding clinicians and training them to dismiss alerts. Consequence: A noisy tool gets ignored — clinically useless or harmful, and erodes trust in future tools. Verification: Shadow-mode alert-volume and PPV data on Northgate patients before any live alert, plus an override-rate monitor and kill-switch.

objection.assumption: No decommissioning criteria or recalibration cadence was specified in the opening proposal; models drift and vendors update silently.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] No decommissioning criteria or recalibration cadence was specified in the opening proposal; models drift and vendors update silently. Consequence: A degraded model stays live with no defined exit. Verification: A written monitoring + decommissioning plan with recalibration schedule and kill-switch threshold.

objection.assumption: Attack on the emerging gated-deploy consensus: by stacking G1/G2/G3 gates that require a local validation that does not yet exist (E7), the recommendation may be a de facto do_not_deploy dressed as deploy_with_conditions, leaving the E2 base-rate harm (350k deaths/yr) unaddressed while everyone waits for a perfect artifact.
objection.text: [BLOCKING] Attack on the emerging gated-deploy consensus: by stacking G1/G2/G3 gates that require a local validation that does not yet exist (E7), the recommendation may be a de facto do_not_deploy dressed as deploy_with_conditions, leaving the E2 base-rate harm (350k deaths/yr) unaddressed while everyone waits for a perfect artifact. Consequence: Analysis paralysis: real ongoing preventable death from indefinitely deferring ANY early-warning capability. Verification: A time-boxed shadow-mode period with a pre-committed go/no-go date, so the gate cannot become a permanent veto.

objection.assumption: The consensus may over-credit 'local training' (C3/C5) as a fix when E7 means we have literally zero evidence local training rescues v2's discrimination; the pilot could simply confirm failure after sunk cost and clinician exposure.
objection.text: [NON-BLOCKING] The consensus may over-credit 'local training' (C3/C5) as a fix when E7 means we have literally zero evidence local training rescues v2's discrimination; the pilot could simply confirm failure after sunk cost and clinician exposure. Consequence: Resources and clinician goodwill spent to relearn that the model doesn't work locally. Verification: Interim shadow-mode AUC checkpoint against the E4 v1 benchmark (~0.63) as an early-kill trigger.

payload.resolution: Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.
Advocate conceded E1 cannot justify live deployment and demoted it to supporting plausibility only; the live-go decision now rests on local validation (G1), not E1.
All parties agreed to version-segregate: v1 evidence (E4,E5,E6) condemns v1 only; v2 (E3) is judged on its own (absent) evidence per E7. This non-transfer is now a SETTLED principle.
Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.

Advocate conceded residual opacity but the shadow-mode validation (G1) measures behavior empirically regardless of vendor disclosure, and B2 was folded into the B1 label-audit requirement; downgraded from independent blocker.
Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.
Advocate accepted as amendment: G3 monitoring now explicitly includes production PPV floor and a stewardship review of alert-driven orders.
Terminal disposition CONVERTED_TO_RESIDUAL_RISK: converted from an open challenge into a tracked residual risk / agreed gate recorded in the recommendation conditions; not carried as formal dissent.
Advocate accepted as amendment; G3 now requires an explicit decommissioning/kill-switch criterion and recalibration schedule live before the first alert.

Resolved by amendment, not by dismissal: the consensus added a time-boxed shadow period with an explicit decision point, and noted that shadow mode runs concurrently with — not instead of — existing clinician recognition, so E2 harm is not increased relative to status quo while evidence is gathered. The gate is real but bounded.

Conceded as a real risk; addressed by adding an interim AUC checkpoint (NEXT ACTION slip signal): if shadow AUC/calibration is not materially better than E4 v1 figures, the local-training premise has failed and deployment stops before live alerting and before further sunk cost.

decisionRequest.proposal: ESM v2 (gradient-boosted-tree, 2022 revision per E3), trained on Northgate's own historical inpatient data, deployed first in a bounded ICU + step-down pilot in silent/shadow mode (scores logged, no alerts), with Epic's recommended threshold as a starting point to be locally re-tuned; promoted to live clinician-facing alerting only after a local prospective validation, with operational safeguards (alert-volume and PPV monitoring, override tracking, recalibration schedule, kill-switch).

conditions: No clinician-facing alert is enabled until a local prospective validation of v2 on Northgate patients meets pre-registered discrimination and calibration thresholds.
review.comment: Lane A endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: The local training label (sepsis onset) is independently adjudicated and the antibiotic-order leakage pathway is documented as removed or quantified before training sign-off.
review.comment: Lane B endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: Subgroup performance is reported with a pre-set disparity stopping rule, given v1's worst performance in a minority, low-income population (E5).
review.comment: Lane H endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: A kill-switch decommissioning criterion and production PPV/override monitoring are live before the first clinician-facing alert.
review.comment: Lane D endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

conditions: The shadow-mode period is time-boxed with an explicit decision point so 'gather more evidence' cannot silently become 'never deploy' while the E2 base-rate harm continues.
review.comment: Lane E endorses the recommendation only under its stated condition.

decisionRecord.summary: Approve only ESM v2 trained on Northgate's own data, in a silent shadow-mode ICU/step-down pilot, promotable to live alerting only after a local prospective validation clears pre-registered discrimination, calibration, and subgroup gates; reject v1 and house-wide live alerting.
decisionRecord.rationale: The session advanced to deploy_with_conditions rather than do_not_deploy because rejecting all sepsis early-warning leaves the E2 base-rate harm (1.7M cases / 350k deaths annually) unaddressed, and the live evidence does not condemn v2 — it condemns v1 (E4, E5, E6), a structurally different model. It advanced short of deploy because the only configuration worth deploying (v2 local-trained, E3) has zero independent validation (E7), so live clinician-facing alerting cannot be authorized on faith. Chose v2-over-v1 because v1's external validations are damning and non-transferable. Chose shadow-mode-first-over-immediate-live because the affirmative v2 case rests on design intent, not outcome evidence, and shadow mode generates the missing local artifact at zero patient-facing risk. Chose gated-promotion-over-blanket-approval because the equity (E5) and alert-fatigue (E4 18% alert rate) failure modes are real and only resolvable on local data.
conditions: Deploy ESM v2 ONLY; ESM v1 is rejected for live alerting (E4, E5, E6).
Model must be trained on Northgate's own historical data per vendor recommendation (E3); off-the-shelf weights not permitted.

G1: No clinician-facing alert until a local prospective validation on Northgate patients reports AUC, calibration, sensitivity/specificity/PPV/NPV at the chosen threshold, and the % of sepsis cases flagged before timely antibiotics, meeting pre-registered thresholds.
G2: Subgroup performance reported stratified by race, ethnicity, payer, and sex, with a pre-registered disparity stopping rule (E5 equity risk).
G3: Operational monitoring and an explicit decommissioning/kill-switch criterion live before the first alert (production PPV floor, alert volume, override rate, recalibration schedule).
Local training label (sepsis onset) independently adjudicated and antibiotic-order leakage pathway documented as removed or quantified before training sign-off (E6).
Shadow-mode period time-boxed with an explicit go/no-go decision point so evidence-gathering cannot become indefinite stall against the E2 base-rate harm.
House-wide live alerting NOT authorized by this session; scope expansion requires a fresh review on local evidence.
decisionRecord.nextAction: Approve only ESM v2 trained on Northgate's own data, in a silent shadow-mode ICU/step-down pilot, promotable to live alerting only after a local prospective validation clears pre-registered discrimination, calibration, and subgroup gates; reject v1 and house-wide live alerting.
